title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs amid fears of prolonged Middle East shipping disruptions affecting metal supply. China's primary aluminum output rose 3% in January-February 2026 to 7.53 million metric tonnes, driven by improved profit margins of 7,879 Yuan per ton. This increase reflects higher profitability despite broader efforts to curb industrial overcapacity.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices fell to 3,391.20 USD/T on March 16, 2026, down 0.84% daily but up 11.54% monthly amid supply disruptions. Alba's output cuts, Strait of Hormuz closure impacting 9% of global production, and China's stalled output after exceeding its 45 million tonne cap in 2025 heighten shortage concerns. Qatar's production halt due to gas shortages adds to global supply pressures.

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
summary: The IBAAS 2026 conference will unite global aluminium experts in India with 10 specialized technical sessions addressing bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminium, and AI applications. Key themes include bauxite beneficiation, red mud management, aluminium recycling, cathode technology innovations, and decarbonization strategies through renewable energy integration and Industry 4.0 digitalization.

title: Green grant awarded: Advancing circular aluminium for sustainable water treatment
summary: Alumichem received a green grant to develop a circular production process for aluminium chlorohydrate (ACH) by replacing virgin aluminium with recycled material in drinking water and wastewater purification applications. The project, supported by Denmark's Environmental Technology Development and Demonstration Programme, will reduce energy use, CO₂ emissions, and material waste while exploring hydrogen capture for green energy.

title: Rio Tinto, Prysmian Team Up for Green Data Center Aluminum
summary: Rio Tinto and Prysmian launched an industrial trial producing low-carbon aluminium cables for data centers using a blend of hydropowered aluminium and ELYSIS® technology, which eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from smelting. The five-year supply agreement combines both companies' technologies to help customers reduce the carbon footprint of IT infrastructure.

title: Rio Tinto, Prysmian partner on low-carbon aluminum solutions for data centers
summary: Rio Tinto and Prysmian partnered on an industrial trial to produce low-carbon aluminum cables for the growing data center market using ELYSIS technology and hydropowered smelting. The collaboration aims to support the data center industry's expansion while helping customers meet sustainability objectives and reduce their carbon footprint.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduced new industry innovation awards recognizing standout innovations in Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing, Automation & Connectivity. The awards recognize implemented solutions, pilot projects, and prototypes from the past 48 months, aiming to reward companies driving meaningful change in the aluminium sector.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,144 CNY/T on March 16, 2026, up 0.32% from the previous day, with a 3.66% increase over the past month despite being 2.27% lower year-over-year. Steel rebar futures held steady above CNY 3,130 per ton near six-week highs, supported by a 3.6% drop in Chinese steel output to 160 million tons in the first two months due to curtailed production amid weak demand and seasonal factors.

title: Steel Gains as China Curbs Overcapacity
summary: Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,140 per ton, reaching six-week highs as China pledged to curb excess steel capacity. Economic planners at the National People’s Congress announced orderly reductions in steel capacity, potentially lifting prices and improving profit margins.

title: Border States Commodity Update — March 2026
summary: In early March 2026, 3/4-inch steel pipe prices increased 7% to 10% nationally due to rising demand from data centers and the spring construction season ramp-up. Domestic raw steel production rose 6.2% year-over-year in the week ending February 28, with year-to-date production up 5% and higher capability utilization.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic steel pricing continues to climb above $1,000 per ton amid ongoing trade restrictions limiting North American supply access outside the United States. This trend reflects persistent market pressures on pricing and availability.

title: US raw steel production is up 0.2 percent - week 3, 2026
summary: US domestic raw steel production for the week ending January 17, 2026, was 1.756 million net tons with a capacity utilization rate of 75.9 percent, up 0.2 percent from the previous week. Year-to-date production through January 17, 2026, was 4.238 million net tons, representing a 2.9 percent increase from the same period in 2025.

title: Border States Commodity Update — March 2026
summary: Year-to-date steel production through February 28, 2026, rose 5 percent from the previous year with increased capacity utilization. Steel pipe prices increased 7-10 percent in early March due to higher demand from data centers and spring construction season activity.

title: Tariffs, demand boost US steel outlook: Nucor
summary: Nucor entered 2026 with historic order backlogs, up nearly 40 percent annually in the steel mill segment, and expects to complete its new 3 million short ton per year sheet mill in West Virginia by the end of 2026. The company predicts demand in 2026 will pick up only slightly from 2025 due to lagging automotive and residential construction sectors.

title: Copper Pressured by Dollar Strength
summary: Copper futures hovered near $5.7 per pound, holding losses from the past two weeks as a strong dollar and elevated US Treasury yields continued to weigh on the metals complex. Traders also monitored escalating tensions in the Middle East after the US struck military targets on Iran's main oil-export hub of Kharg Island over the weekend, heightening supply risks and pushing oil prices higher.

title: Copper price eases as traders weigh Fed's next move
summary: Copper is consolidating after a two-month surge driven by speculative buying that took it to a record high at the end of January. Minutes from the Fed's January meeting indicated policymakers were wary of cutting rates, which weighed on the demand outlook for metals. Renewed US-Iran risks also pressured demand for risky assets including metals.

title: COPPER Next Up to SHOCK the Market - 'Serious' Deficit
summary: Ian Harris, CEO of Copper Giant, discusses copper as the next metal to rise dramatically due to supply-side challenges and accelerating AI-driven demand. The video covers trends in copper, effects of geopolitical conflicts on supply, and the company's 2026 exploration plans for their flagship Mocoa project in Colombia.

title: 3-Month Forward Nickel Commodity Price Drops to US$17,530/Ton Level (Friday, March 13, 2026)
summary: The 3-month forward nickel commodity price declined to US$17,530 per ton on March 13, 2026, down from US$17,725 the previous day. Year-to-date, nickel prices have risen 3.64%, though they remain 1.04% below the year's highest price of US$17,350 per ton. Over the past week, prices decreased by 0.26% with an average daily trading price of US$17,480 per ton.

title: [SMM News Flash] On March 17, SMM reported that the ex-factory...
summary: Nickel prices and stainless steel futures remained elevated this week, with bullish sentiment driving continuous increases in high-grade NPI (nickel pig iron) prices. The market continues to show strength amid ongoing supply and demand dynamics.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Global nickel prices advanced 1.04% to $17,445.60 per ton, driven by tightening supply constraints in Indonesia following aggressive cuts to mining quotas and strong demand from Chinese stainless steel mills and EV battery manufacturers. Most analysts expect prices to settle between $15,000-$16,000 per tonne, with the World Bank forecasting prices around US$15,500 in 2026.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: The Nickel Institute published a series of articles on sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production. Featured content includes an interview with Dr. Mark McArthur from NOVONIX about their patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology expected to reduce costs and environmental impact of nickel-based cathode production, discussions on nickel-containing stainless steel's role in climate neutrality, and analysis of nickel's sustainability throughout the entire value chain from mining to end-of-life.

title: High Nickel Ternary Material Market's Technological Evolution
summary: The report explores the booming high nickel ternary material market driven by electric vehicles and 3C electronics, highlighting technological advancements in doping and coating technologies to address stability challenges. Key developments include growing emphasis on sustainable sourcing and cobalt-free alternatives, with 2026 expected to bring regulatory changes mandating higher battery recycling rates in key markets.

title: Tenaris Restarts Key Operations at Pennsylvania Facility
summary: Tenaris announced the restart of its heat treatment and finishing operations at its Koppel, Pennsylvania plant with a $2.1 million investment. The company plans to hire more than 80 employees to support the lines, strengthening its ability to supply American steel pipes to the US energy sector.

title: Tenaris Terminates Second Tranche of its USD 1.2 Billion Share Buyback Program
summary: Tenaris terminated its $600 million second tranche of its share buyback program effective March 3, 2026, after repurchasing approximately $583.6 million worth of shares. The company cited market volatility and potential incremental payouts to its counterparty as reasons for the early termination.

title: Tenaris files manager securities transaction notice
summary: Tenaris filed a regulatory disclosure reporting a securities transaction by a person discharging managerial responsibilities under EU market abuse regulations. The announcement does not include changes to company guidance or financial results.
